core_payload.content:
# API Gateway

## 1. Role
API Gateway is one of the hardest sovereignty surfaces in N1Hub. Every provider call, hosted-inference request, webhook callback, connector exchange, and background agent egress passes through a boundary that is lane-aware, redactable, auditable, and economically governed.

## 2. Boundary Contract
- richer route catalogs for model providers, hosted inference, connectors, and callbacks
- actor-bound authentication and authorization at the boundary itself
- lane-aware rate and budget control for interactive, background, and hosted traffic
- stronger observability and artifact lineage for every boundary crossing
- policy hooks for redaction, review, compliance, and trust enforcement

## 3. Operational Consequence
External capability deepens without becoming opaque because outside power remains searchable, reviewable, and constrained by explicit policy rather than disappearing into scripts or intuition.

## 4. Philosophical Alignment
API Gateway supports To Dig Deep by making openness powerful without weakening control.

neuro_concentrate.summary: API Gateway defines one of the hardest sovereignty surfaces in N1Hub: the boundary through which provider calls, hosted inference, webhooks, connector exchanges, and background-agent egress are authenticated, observed, rate-governed, and audited. It covers actor-bound authorization, lane-aware economics, stronger observability, artifact lineage, and policy hooks for redaction, review, compliance, and trust enforcement. The capsule matters because external capability can deepen without becoming opaque when every boundary crossing remains searchable, reviewable, and constrained by explicit policy rather than dissolving into scripts or operator intuition.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Removed meta-language so the capsule states current doctrine directly.
